name: Yamaha MG10XU live vocal small-room setup

setup.gain: Raise gain until the loudest vocal peaks cleanly without clipping.
setup.eq: Roll off low rumble, keep mids controlled, add a little presence only if the vocal sounds dull.
setup.fx: Use a light hall or plate reverb and keep it subtle.
setup.monitor: Keep the mic behind the mains and notch problem frequencies before adding more level.
setup.notes: Good default for spoken announcements or lead vocal in a compact room.

llmSummary: Use this as a starting point for a clean live vocal on a Yamaha MG10XU in a small room.

name: Bose T8S solo vocal karaoke handheld setup

setup.toneMatch: ToneMatch preset Vocal Mics > Handheld Mics.
setup.gain: Set preamp conservatively and leave headroom for louder singers.
setup.eq: Start flat, cut mud before boosting highs, and use built-in vocal shaping carefully.
setup.fx: Use a short vocal reverb or delay so the singer feels supported without losing clarity.
setup.monitor: Keep speakers in front of the mic and watch for feedback if singers cup the microphone.
setup.notes: Useful for rotating singers where consistency matters more than a perfect individual mix. Derived from the Bose T4S/T8S owner guide vocal and karaoke-style starting points.

llmSummary: This is a stable handheld vocal starting point for a Bose T8S with portable speakers and the built-in Handheld Mics ToneMatch preset.

name: Bose T8S headworn vocal small-room setup

setup.toneMatch: ToneMatch preset Vocal Mics > Headworn Mics.
setup.gain: Bring trim up carefully because headworn mics can jump in level fast when the talker projects.
setup.eq: Keep low end tidy and reduce harsh upper mids before adding more brightness.
setup.fx: Keep effects dry or nearly dry for spoken word and announcements.
setup.monitor: Use speaker placement first and keep headworn users out of the main horn pattern before adding more level.
setup.notes: Best starting point for spoken-word work, hosts, and presenters when clarity matters more than size.

llmSummary: Headworn vocal starting construct for the Bose T8S using the built-in Headworn Mics preset.

name: Bose L1 Pro8 front of house small-room setup
speakerConfig: two column arrays as front of house

setup.system: Run the pair as the main front of house system and keep the upstream mix balanced before reshaping the speakers themselves.
setup.gain: Bring the system up until speech and music cover the room cleanly without leaning on the limiters.
setup.eq: Start flat, trim harsh upper mids only if the room gets edgy, and avoid heavy low boosts until boundary buildup is checked.
setup.monitor: If performers need more level, use a dedicated monitor path instead of pushing the front of house columns harder.
setup.placement: Place the columns slightly ahead of the microphones, keep them wide enough for even coverage, and aim for coverage before loudness.
setup.notes: Useful starting point when the L1 Pro8 pair is carrying the room and the mixer needs to feed a clean, even front-of-house image.

llmSummary: Front-of-house speaker-system starting construct for a Bose L1 Pro8 pair in a compact room.

name: Bose T8S acoustic guitar piezo small-room setup

setup.toneMatch: ToneMatch preset Acoustic Guitars > Steel String w/ piezo.
setup.gain: Set trim from real strumming peaks, not gentle picking, so harder hits stay clean.
setup.eq: Use the preset as the base, then trim low-mid boom before adding sparkle.
setup.fx: Start with a light chorus or short reverb only if the guitar feels too dry in the room.
setup.monitor: Watch the low mids first if the guitar starts to feed back onstage.
setup.notes: Matches the owner guide singer-songwriter scene starting point for steel-string acoustic guitar with pickup.

llmSummary: Built-in Bose acoustic guitar starting point centered on the Steel String w/ piezo ToneMatch preset.

name: Bose T8S keyboard general keys small-room setup

setup.gain: Set trim from your strongest performance patch so layer changes do not suddenly clip.
setup.eq: Keep the low end under control and carve space for vocals before boosting top end.
setup.fx: Leave the channel mostly dry unless the keyboard patch itself is too stark.
setup.monitor: Keep stereo sources balanced and check that summed mono monitor feeds do not get muddy.
setup.notes: Matches the owner guide singer-songwriter scene for keyboard support.

llmSummary: General keyboard starting construct for the Bose T8S using the built-in General Keys preset.

name: Bose T8S bass active bass 1 small-room setup

setup.gain: Set trim while the bassist digs in so the low end stays solid without flattening the channel.
setup.eq: Keep sub buildup controlled and define the attack in the low mids before adding extra highs.
setup.fx: Stay mostly dry unless a song-specific effect is part of the bass sound.
setup.monitor: Let stage volume carry bass where possible instead of overfilling wedges.
setup.notes: Matches the owner guide full-band scene starting point for bass guitar.

llmSummary: Active bass starting construct for the Bose T8S using the built-in Active Bass 1 preset.

name: Bose T8S electric guitar SM57 amp small-room setup

setup.toneMatch: ToneMatch preset Electric Guitars > Mic'd Amp w/ SM57.
setup.gain: Set trim from the loudest chorus or solo level, not just rhythm playing.
setup.eq: Use the preset first, then trim harsh upper mids before adding more bite.
setup.fx: Keep time-based effects from the amplifier or pedals in mind before adding more in the mixer.
setup.monitor: Watch amp spill before pushing guitar hard into stage monitors.
setup.notes: Matches the owner guide full-band scene for electric guitar with an SM57 on the amp.

llmSummary: Electric guitar amp starting construct for the Bose T8S using the Mic'd Amp w/ SM57 preset.

name: Bose T8S kick drum general small-room setup

setup.gain: Set trim from the hardest kick hits so the channel has punch without clipping.
setup.eq: Let the preset carry the shape first, then clean the low mids before adding more click.
setup.fx: Keep the channel dry unless the room or production style really needs extra ambience.
setup.monitor: Use KickGate or careful thresholding before pushing more kick into the PA.
setup.notes: Based on the owner guide percussion and full-band examples. Good starting point before engaging KickGate.

llmSummary: Kick drum starting construct for the Bose T8S using the Kick, General preset.

name: Bose T8S overhead percussion small-room setup

setup.gain: Set trim from cymbal crashes and snare accents so overhead peaks stay open but controlled.
setup.eq: Reduce brittle highs before adding more snap, and keep lower mids from clouding the kit.
setup.fx: Start dry and let the room or drum ambience mics create the sense of space.
setup.monitor: Avoid overfeeding overheads into wedges unless the stage really needs them.
setup.notes: Matches the owner guide full-band scene overhead starting point.

llmSummary: Overhead drum starting construct for the Bose T8S using the General Overhead preset.

name: Bose T8S DJ playback small-room setup

setup.toneMatch: ToneMatch preset DJ/Playback > Flat, zEQ Controls.
setup.gain: Set source trim from the hottest playback content so transitions do not clip the channel.
setup.eq: Start flat and use zEQ only to fit the room or speaker system, not to remake the track.
setup.fx: Keep channel effects off for playback sources unless the show has a deliberate effect moment.
setup.monitor: Maintain left-right balance and watch low-end buildup in small rooms.
setup.notes: Matches the owner guide DJ/Playback scene for stereo program material.

llmSummary: Playback starting construct for the Bose T8S using the DJ/Playback preset.

name: Small club vocal band venue preset
speakerConfig: two mains on stands with conservative wedges

setup.system: Keep mains just ahead of the vocal mics, keep subs controlled, and favor even coverage over loudness.
setup.gain: Set gains for the loudest song in soundcheck and leave margin for the room getting louder once it fills.
setup.eq: Trim low-mid buildup quickly because small club rooms stack mud before they feel exciting.
setup.monitor: Keep wedges simple and vocal-forward so stage level does not outrun the room.
setup.placement: Use speaker placement to solve feedback first because room reflections arrive fast in compact bars.
setup.notes: Venue preset for compact bar rooms where spill, reflections, and vocal clarity decide the night.

llmSummary: Small club venue preset centered on fast vocal intelligibility and restrained stage volume.

name: Festival stage turnover venue preset

setup.system: Verify stage patch, matrices, and speaker processing before trusting any recalled scene.
setup.gain: Treat festival gains as living values because guest engineers and line checks change the real level picture all day.
setup.monitor: Keep monitor sends labeled and minimal so changeovers stay readable under pressure.
setup.placement: Confirm walkways, fills, and side coverage before doors because big stages hide blind spots.
setup.notes: Venue preset for fast changeovers where routing certainty matters more than fine tone moves during the first minute.

llmSummary: Festival stage venue preset built for repeatable routing, safe recall habits, and quick verification.

name: Conference room speech venue preset

setup.system: Favor even speech coverage and low-stress routing over dramatic volume or effects.
setup.gain: Set panel and host gains for comfortable speech peaks, not applause or laptop playback spikes.
setup.eq: Trim low-end boundary buildup and keep presence moves modest so the room does not turn brittle.
setup.fx: Keep effects dry because clarity is the product here.
setup.notes: Venue preset for panels, meetings, and spoken-word events where intelligibility outranks spectacle.

llmSummary: Conference room venue preset tuned for even coverage, dry speech, and predictable operation.

name: Studio control room reference preset

setup.system: Keep monitoring at a repeatable reference level and confirm mono or single-speaker checks are easy to access.
setup.eq: Do not chase room compensation during a tired session; use references before making broad tone calls.
setup.placement: Preserve a stable listening triangle and avoid shifting the desk setup between sessions if possible.
setup.notes: Venue preset for regaining perspective in a control room before making heavier mix decisions.

llmSummary: Studio control room venue preset focused on stable listening level, references, and perspective resets.

name: Karaoke bar rotation venue preset

setup.system: Keep playback and host routing simple because the room changes singers faster than it changes hardware.
setup.gain: Leave generous headroom for singers who suddenly get louder or hold the mic too close.
setup.eq: Use broad survivability moves, not custom vocal shaping for one singer.
setup.monitor: Keep any host monitor low and stable so the room stays easy to manage when mics move unpredictably.
setup.notes: Venue preset for karaoke nights where consistency, speed, and feedback survival beat perfection.

llmSummary: Karaoke bar venue preset built around fast singer changes, safe headroom, and stable playback balance.
